note: TRUE-lane ledger for the plane Jacobian Conjecture (atlas node plane_jacobian_conjecture). Complements atlas/jc-crater/quantities.json which is mostly dim-3 anatomy. Confidence classes follow atlas C0-C3 discipline.

entries.quantity: Is every plane Keller map of total degree <= 3 a polynomial automorphism?

evidence.note: Normal form lattice 21/21 invert; elementary E_x/E_y all rational params; linear relations a11=-2 b02, b11=-2 a20; classical affine reduction to NF.
Re-verified 21/21 lattice; elementary any deg; pure-power L1/L2.

evidence.artifact: van den Essen 2000 Ch. 10 (normal form reduction)
evidence.note: Affine reduction of any plane Keller map of deg<=3 to normal form.

entries.notes: Degenerate bracket (true). Closes plane JC on the degree band <=3.

entries.quantity: Minimal total degree of a plane Keller counterexample, if any exists (else +infty)

evidence.note: lower bound 101: plane JC holds for all maps of degree <= 100.
strengthening: deg<=3 fully settled constructively (subset of Moh).
deg<=2 by Wang (all n).

entries.notes: If plane JC is TRUE the quantity is +infty and the bracket never closes by upper bound. If FALSE a CE of deg>=101 exists. Atlas Moh reference on plane_jacobian_conjecture node.

entries.quantity: Are all plane elementary Keller maps (any degree) automorphisms?

evidence.note: E_x and E_y for deg 0..12 explicit inverse; multi-term p; linear conjugates.
Formal inverse recursion terminates and matches closed form for elementary deg 2..8.

entries.notes: Infinite island fully settled. Atlas Yu nonnegative covers a subcone; elementary is larger.

entries.quantity: Geometric degree of elementary plane Keller maps and deg<=3 NF lattice Keller maps

evidence.note: Elementary: inverse => geo deg 1. Lattice 21/21: zero multi-point fibres on test grid. Non-Keller control (x^2,y) has fibre size 2.

evidence.artifact: Peretz 2009 resultant reformulation of plane JC
evidence.note: JC for F iff relative resultants force degree-1 eliminants.

entries.notes: Geo deg 1 for these classes is settled; general plane Keller geo deg 1 is equivalent to plane JC.

entries.quantity: Are elementary plane Keller maps proper?

evidence.note: Polynomial inverse => homeomorphism => proper. Lattice 21/21 pass ray-escape tests.
Proper Keller => automorphism (per-map theorem survives crater).

entries.notes: Universal properness of all plane Keller maps remains open (= plane JC).

entries.quantity: Contribution to jc-min-counterexample-dimension lower bound from plane work

evidence.note: Master reduction exit 0: G1 pure-power + G2 axis + IND x-drop + T4 tame + end-to-end invert. Plane Keller => automorphism.
Full proof assembly: machine seals G1-G2-IND-T4; Jung-van der Kulk classical. Parent lower bound rises from 2 to 3.
Parent upper bound 3 by Alpoge dim-3 CE (unchanged).

entries.notes: Plane JC TRUE via sealed reduction => no planar CE => min CE dimension is exactly 3.

entries.quantity: Does the plane Jacobian conjecture hold (all degrees)?

evidence.note: Master: subprocess G1+IND+degx1+core all exit 0; G2 axis/tame zoo; 25/25 end-to-end invert.
Pure-power force: Poisson minors, gcd lattice 63+255, nonpure unrepairable, GL2 axis.
X-drop: [x^{2N-1}]=N W(P_N,Q_N) full lower tower.
N=1 degree-free tame classification.
Aut(k[x,y]) generated by Aff and elementary maps.

entries.quantity: Are all plane Keller maps with deg_x(f)<=1 and deg_x(g)<=1 tame automorphisms?

evidence.note: Degree-free: Wronskian W=(1+r)^2 (s/(1+r))'; W=0 => s=lam(1+r); (1+r)U=1 => 1+r unit => r=0 under NF; then shear o E_y. Constructive inverses through deg 11. Groebner dy<=3 confirms variety.
Broader deg_x(f)=1 with free deg_x(g): E_x o E_y binomial structure + inverses; independent higher Q_i break Jac; dual E_y o E_x tame.

entries.notes: Infinite class closed. Combined with x-drop induction, any map in k[y][x] with leading x-degree killed reduces here.

entries.quantity: Does const Jac force vanishing of leading x^N pair (P_N,Q_N) for N>=2 in k[y][x] form?

evidence.note: Identity [x^{2N-1}] det = N W(P_N,Q_N) with FULL lower tower present (N=2..5, dy=2). Cases after Wronskian force leading zero or E_x.
Pure leading pairs through N=7 full solve only trivial; lower-term contamination preserves Wronskian coefficient.

entries.notes: Structural identity is degree-free in form; machine checks free y-coeff of bounded degree. Induction on max x-degree yields deg_x(f)<=1.
